stem: A agriculture study measures crop yield across 4 regions, each containing multiple farms. region A has 15 observations with mean 79.0 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 4.5). region B has 20 observations with mean 80.8 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 6.5). region C has 8 observations with mean 73.6 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 5.2). region D has 10 observations with mean 75.7 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 4.0). The between-region standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 7.8 and 19.5 tonnes/hectare. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true crop yield for region C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-region variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for region C: 73.7 tonnes/hectare (shrunk from raw 73.6). Range [73.6, 73.8] as between-region SD varies from 7.8 to 19.5. Pooled mean: 78.2.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 78.2 tonnes/hectare (weighted average of 4 region means).
Shrinkage factor for region C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/8). At σ_between=13.0: B = 0.98.
Interval [73.6, 73.8] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (73.6) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (78.2) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
